title: Sequences, expressions and formulae

atomic_scrolls.title: In a linear sequence
atomic_scrolls.raw_text: In a linear sequence the terms in the sequence can increase or decrease by the same amount each time.

atomic_scrolls.title: Worked example 2.1
atomic_scrolls.raw_text:
a The first term of a sequence is 4. The term-to-term rule of the sequence is 'subtract 3'. Write down the first three terms of the sequence.
b The position-to-term rule of a sequence is: term = 4 × position number + 1. Work out the first three terms of the sequence.

a First three terms are 4, 1, –2.
Write down the first term, which is 4, then use the term-to-term rule to work out the second and third terms.
2nd term = 4 – 3 = 1, 3rd term = 1 – 3 = –2.
b First three terms are 5, 9, 13.
Use the position-to-term rule to work out each term.
1st term = 4 × 1 + 1 = 5, 2nd term = 4 × 2 + 1 = 9, 3rd term = 4 × 3 + 1 = 13.
atomic_scrolls.latex:
a The first term of a sequence is 4. The term-to-term rule of the sequence is 'subtract 3'. Write down the first three terms of the sequence.
b The position-to-term rule of a sequence is: $ \text{term} = 4 \times \text{position number} + 1 $. Work out the first three terms of the sequence.

a First three terms are 4, 1, –2.
Write down the first term, which is 4, then use the term-to-term rule to work out the second and third terms.
$ 2\text{nd term} = 4 - 3 = 1 $, $ 3\text{rd term} = 1 - 3 = -2 $.
b First three terms are 5, 9, 13.
Use the position-to-term rule to work out each term.
$ 1\text{st term} = 4 \times 1 + 1 = 5 $, $ 2\text{nd term} = 4 \times 2 + 1 = 9 $, $ 3\text{rd term} = 4 \times 3 + 1 = 13 $.

questions.raw_text: Write down the first three terms of each sequence.
The first term of a sequence is 15. The term-to-term rule is add 7. What is the fifth term of the sequence? Explain how you worked out your answer.

This is part of Zalika’s homework.
a Is Zalika’s answer correct?
b Explain how her method works.
c The first term of a sequence is 5. The term-to-term rule is ‘add 8’. Use Zalika’s method to work out:
i the 10th term
ii the 20th term
iii the 50th term of the sequence.

questions.raw_text: Ahmad is trying to solve this problem. Work out the answer to the problem. Explain how you solved the problem.
Mia is trying to solve this problem. Work out the answer to the problem. Explain how you solved the problem.

The table shows two of the terms in a sequence. Position-to-term rule: term = position number + 7 Copy the table. Use the position-to-term rule to work out the missing numbers.

Position number | 1 | 2 | 3 | 4 | 5 | 10 | 20
Term | | 9 | | | | | 17
questions.latex:
The table shows two of the terms in a sequence. Position-to-term rule: $ \text{term} = \text{position number} + 7 $. Copy the table. Use the position-to-term rule to work out the missing numbers.

$ \begin{array}{|l|c|c|c|c|c|c|c|}
\hline
\text{Position number} & 1 & 2 & 3 & 4 & 5 & 10 & 20 \\
\hline
\text{Term} & & 9 & & & & & 17 \\
\hline
\end{array} $

b term = position number – 4

d term = 3 × position number – 1

questions.raw_text: The fourth term of a sequence is 11. The seventh term of the sequence is 20. Which of these position-to-term rules is the correct one for the sequence? A term = position number + 7 B term = 2 × position number + 3 C term = 3 × position number – 1 D term = 4 × position number – 5 Show how you worked out your answer.
questions.latex:
The fourth term of a sequence is 11. The seventh term of the sequence is 20. Which of these position-to-term rules is the correct one for the sequence?
A $ \text{term} = \text{position number} + 7 $ 
B $ \text{term} = 2 \times \text{position number} + 3 $ 
C $ \text{term} = 3 \times \text{position number} - 1 $ 
D $ \text{term} = 4 \times \text{position number} - 5 $ 
Show how you worked out your answer.

questions.raw_text:
The third term of a sequence is 9. The eighth term of the sequence is 19. Which of these position-to-term rules is the correct one for the sequence? Show how you worked out your answer.
A term = position number + 9
B term = 2 × position number + 3
C term = 3 × position number
D term = 4 × position number – 3

b 6, 11, 16, 21, ...
c 8, 9, 10, 11, ...

questions.raw_text:
This pattern is made from dots.
Pattern 1 [image: three rows of 4, 3, 2, 1 dots, making a triangle; then an additional row of 3 dots]
Pattern 2 [image: three rows of 5, 4, 3, 2, 1 dots, making a triangle; then an additional row of 3 dots]
Pattern 3 [image: three rows of 6, 5, 4, 3, 2, 1 dots, making a triangle; then an additional row of 3 dots]
Pattern 4 [image: three rows of 7, 6, 5, 4, 3, 2, 1 dots, making a triangle; then an additional row of 3 dots]
Alicia thinks that the nth term for the sequence of numbers of dots is 3n + 3. Is Alicia correct? Explain how you worked out your answer.
questions.latex:
This pattern is made from dots.
Pattern 1 [image: three rows of 4, 3, 2, 1 dots, making a triangle; then an additional row of 3 dots]
Pattern 2 [image: three rows of 5, 4, 3, 2, 1 dots, making a triangle; then an additional row of 3 dots]
Pattern 3 [image: three rows of 6, 5, 4, 3, 2, 1 dots, making a triangle; then an additional row of 3 dots]
Pattern 4 [image: three rows of 7, 6, 5, 4, 3, 2, 1 dots, making a triangle; then an additional row of 3 dots]
Alicia thinks that the $n$th term for the sequence of numbers of dots is $3n + 3$. Is Alicia correct? Explain how you worked out your answer.

questions.raw_text:
Copy and complete the table of values for each function machine.
i [function machine: x → +9 → y]
x | 1 | 2 | 8
y | | 14 |
ii [function machine: x → ×2 → –3 → y]
x | 1 | 2
y | | 7 | 19
questions.latex:
Copy and complete the table of values for each function machine.
i [function machine: $ x \rightarrow +9 \rightarrow y $]
$ \begin{array}{|c|c|c|c|}
\hline
x & 1 & 2 & 8 \\
\hline
y & & 14 & \\
\hline
\end{array} $
ii [function machine: $ x \rightarrow \times 2 \rightarrow -3 \rightarrow y $]
$ \begin{array}{|c|c|c|}
\hline
x & 1 & 2 \\
\hline
y & & 7 \quad 19 \\
\hline
\end{array} $

questions.raw_text:
Ahmad thinks of a number, x. Write an expression for the number Ahmad gets when he:
a multiplies the number by 4
b multiplies the number by 2 then adds 7
c divides the number by 3 then subtracts 10
d adds 4 to the number then multiplies by 5.
questions.latex:
Ahmad thinks of a number, $x$. Write an expression for the number Ahmad gets when he:
a multiplies the number by 4
b multiplies the number by 2 then adds 7
c divides the number by 3 then subtracts 10
d adds 4 to the number then multiplies by 5.

parts.raw_text: multiplies the number by 2 then adds 7
divides the number by 3 then subtracts 10
adds 4 to the number then multiplies by 5.

questions.raw_text:
Work out the value of each expression.
a x + 7 when x = –12
b 3x – 4y when x = 2 and y = 7
c 3n + 1 when n = –6
d 30 – 5k when k = –2
e x^2 – 10 when x = 3
f 4y^2 when y = 2
questions.latex:
Work out the value of each expression.
a $x + 7$ when $x = -12$
b $3x - 4y$ when $x = 2$ and $y = 7$
c $3n + 1$ when $n = -6$
d $30 - 5k$ when $k = -2$
e $x^2 - 10$ when $x = 3$
f $4y^2$ when $y = 2$

questions.raw_text: Use the formula P = mgh to work out P when m = 5, g = 10 and h = 3.
questions.latex: Use the formula $P = mgh$ to work out $P$ when $m = 5$, $g = 10$ and $h = 3$.

questions.raw_text:
Zalika uses the formula V = u^2 + 2as.
When she substitutes u = 4, a = 3 and s = 7 into the formula she gets the equation 8 = 6 + 14s. Is Zalika correct? Show how you worked out your answer.
questions.latex:
Zalika uses the formula $V = u^2 + 2as$.
When she substitutes $u = 4$, $a = 3$ and $s = 7$ into the formula she gets the equation $8 = 6 + 14s$. Is Zalika correct? Show how you worked out your answer.
